Apply to adopt

The application is a filter.

We are looking for prepared adopters, not the highest number of applications. If that turns some people away, it is working.

Start application Ask first

Before you apply

Read this like a job description for your home.

A beautiful rescue story does not mean the dog is ready for a beautiful first week. New home, new smells, new people, new rules: to the dog, all of it is new.

You understand decompression. A new home does not mean the dog is ready for parties, road trips, or a camera-perfect first week.

You can afford the dog after the adoption fee. Food, vet care, training, and emergencies are part of saying yes.

You are ready for a hard application. The process includes an application, phone interview, home check, references, and a meet-and-greet when it makes sense.

You will listen when the rescue says a dog is not the right fit. The right match matters more than the fast match.

Path to home

What happens after you apply.

  1. 01

    Application

    Tell the truth. The right details help us protect the dog and your household.

  2. 02

    Phone interview

    We talk through behavior, routine, deal-breakers, and fit.

  3. 03

    Home check

    The home setup has to match the dog in front of us.

  4. 04

    References

    Vet and personal references help confirm readiness.

  5. 05

    Meet-and-greet

    Only when the match looks real do we introduce the dog.

  6. 06

    Adoption fee and handoff

    The support does not end when the dog goes home.

First-party application

Apply for the dog in front of you.

This is the filter before the phone call. Tell the truth about your home, your limits, and the kind of dog you can responsibly handle.

01Applicant

Use the contact information you check fastest.

02Home

The home setup has to match the dog, not the other way around.

List species, age, sex, and whether they are fixed.

03Fit

Power-breed rescue asks more from the adopter. Be specific.

Think crate, quiet space, visitors, walks, car rides, kids, and other pets.

Examples: cats, small dogs, toddlers, leash reactivity, medical needs, separation anxiety.

04References and agreements

This prepares a clean email packet to Adopt@underdogheroes.org so the rescue gets your answers in one place.

Underdog Heroes

Riverside, California rescue for power-breed dogs most people already counted out.

Adopt: Adopt@underdogheroes.org Foster: Foster@underdogheroes.org Volunteer: info@underdogheroes.org Instagram
